name: ETH State Change Momentum Hold v1
thesis: ETH 1h is the best-fit primary lane for a state-change momentum test because the live basket moved to a faster lane and the regime context shows recent trend-down pressure with transitional instability. The edge should come from post-break hold after momentum improves, not from raw breakout touching alone.
invalidation_condition: Abort if the hold logic still cannot generate enough blind-window participation, if momentum and hold filters conflict, or if continuation collapses after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Short-term regime improvement after directional pressure, where price breaks back through structure and then holds instead of instantly mean reverting.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When ETH 1h transitions out of a local down-pressure state, the first accepted reclaim can produce several bars of continuation before balance fully reasserts.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is the cleanest lane for this mechanism because state changes and post-break hold are inherently faster than 4h and should produce better density.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains one-way trend-down without real regime improvement, reclaim holds will fail quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries appear but continuation is too brief, shorten the holding logic before tightening the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; momentum improvement and structural hold are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced by using broad hold logic instead of exact breakout candle requirements

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
